meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome no version control. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first project.
content.answer_block: Eliminate the chaos of manual file management by integrating a robust SaaS starter kit. By implementing automated Git workflows and structured boilerplate architecture, you secure your codebase, enable seamless branching, and establish professional version control, ensuring your student project scales into a production-ready application without the risk of data loss.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of realizing you’ve overwritten your last three days of code is a rite of passage every developer fears. You’re building your first SaaS, the momentum is high, but without version control, your project is a house of cards waiting for a single 'save' mistake to collapse. The difference between a hobbyist and a professional isn't just the code—it's the system that protects it.

Today, we stop building in the dark. We are deploying a professional SaaS starter kit that enforces Git best practices from the first commit. By leveraging Antigravity for structural integrity and Claude Code for automated command execution, we’ll move you from 'file-saving-anxiety' to a professional developer workflow in under an hour.

steps.title: Initialize Repository with Starter Kit
steps.description: Clone a battle-tested SaaS boilerplate that includes pre-configured Git hooks and CI/CD pipelines to ensure you never work without version control again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modern SaaS starter kit using Next.js 14, Tailwind, and Prisma. Ensure the output includes a .gitignore file, a README with setup instructions, and a pre-configured .git directory.

steps.title: Configure Automated Git Hooks
steps.description: Implement Husky to run linting and type-checking before every commit, preventing 'broken' code from ever entering your history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Husky and lint-staged to run 'eslint' and 'tsc --noEmit' on all staged files. Add a pre-commit hook that prevents commits if these checks fail.

steps.title: Establish Branching Strategy
steps.description: Create a 'main' and 'develop' workflow. This protects your stable code from experimental features while you learn.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a branching strategy document for a solo developer. Explain how to use 'git flow' style naming for features and hotfixes.

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Use AI to generate commit messages that actually explain the 'why' behind your changes, making your portfolio history look senior-level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that parses the last 5 file changes and generates a professional, conventional-commits compliant commit message.

steps.title: Deploy to Version-Controlled Cloud
steps.description: Connect your repository to Vercel or GitHub Actions so that every push triggers a build, proving your app is always deployable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs 'npm run build' and 'npm run test' on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, refactor:) to make your Git history searchable for future employers.
Treat your local 'node_modules' as disposable; if it isn't in package.json, it doesn't exist.
Use a 'feature-first' folder structure inside your starter kit to keep your code modular and easier to refactor later.
Configure your IDE to show Git blame annotations; it helps you track exactly when a specific bug was introduced.
Never push secrets to GitHub; use .env.example files to define your environment variables without exposing sensitive keys.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is secured, push your first 'feature' branch to GitHub and open a Pull Request to yourself. This simulates the professional code review process used in top-tier tech companies.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own files and start building like a professional. You have the tools, the strategy, and the workflow to scale your ideas. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
